summaries.52599: Comments confirm the issue affects Android only; iOS keeps the keyboard visible on resume. The root cause is that the framework does not send a `show` message to the Android text input plugin when the app returns to the foreground, because `EditableText` does not respond to app lifecycle changes and Android app lifecycle changes do not trigger in-app focus changes. A related observation is that simply dragging down and releasing the notifications panel also causes keyboard loss on some Samsung devices (Android 14) but not on Pixel devices running Android 10. The issue is currently stalled with no assignee and no open PR.
summaries.83611: Comments confirm reproduction on Chrome desktop. The root cause is identified as the framework lacking a concept of blurring a text field without simultaneously focusing something else: when the user taps a platform view, the browser moves focus into the platform view but Flutter still considers the previous text field focused. The issue was escalated to P1 and noted as related to broader platform-view focus design, but has no fix or open PR and the assignee was removed.
summaries.113253: Comments confirm the bug is Android-only and device-specific: reproducible on Samsung Galaxy M51 (Android 11, Gboard) but not on Pixel 7 Pro. The root cause is excessive `_syncWithOS` calls that bounce editing state back to the engine even when changes originated in the engine, causing stale text to appear in `TextEditingDeltaNonTextUpdate` and triggering an assertion (`range.end is out of text of length`). The suggested fix was to limit `_syncWithOS` calls to cases where the framework is directly mutating editing state; applying this in super_editor's own IME code reduced occurrences. The issue is currently stalled with no open PR in the Flutter repo and the assignee was removed.
